description.p1: According to the EIA, in 2024 the average daily flow of crude oil and petroleum products through the Strait of Hormuz was 20 million barrels — roughly 20% of global petroleum consumption.
description.p2: About 20% of global LNG trade also passed through the strait, with 84% of crude and condensate and 83% of LNG heading to Asian markets.
description.p3: Any disruption to energy supply through the Strait of Hormuz can have a major impact on global industry and economies. This project was created to bring all that information together in one place, rather than searching across multiple sources.
description.p4: Hormuz Monitor is a dashboard that lets you see vessel movements through the Strait of Hormuz, oil prices, and key U.S. market indices at a glance. It is designed to help you access scattered information faster, in one place.

sources.heading: Data Sources
sources.list: IMF PortWatch, MarineTraffic, EIA, TradingView, Yahoo Finance, Al Jazeera RSS, BBC RSS, Anadolu Agency RSS

notice.peace: We hope the war ends soon and peace comes to everyone.
